error.signature: Looking up a deactivated widget's ancestor is unsafe.
error.regex: (Looking up a deactivated widget|deactivated widget's ancestor|This error happens.*BuildContext.*after.*dispose|setState.*deactivated)

verdict.summary: BuildContext used after the widget has been removed from the tree. Commonly occurs when navigating or showing dialogs from async callbacks.

dead_ends.action: Store BuildContext in a variable and use it later in async code
dead_ends.why_fails: Context becomes invalid after the widget is deactivated; storing it does not keep it alive

dead_ends.action: Use a GlobalKey to get context from anywhere
dead_ends.why_fails: GlobalKey.currentContext can also be null after widget disposal; same lifecycle issue

workarounds.action: Check mounted before using context after an await
Use a NavigatorKey or router at the app level for navigation from async code
workarounds.how: final navigatorKey = GlobalKey<NavigatorState>(); // set on MaterialApp, use navigatorKey.currentState!.push(...)

workarounds.action: Move async logic to a provider/bloc and react to state changes in the widget
workarounds.how: Widget listens to state stream and navigates in build/listener, not in async callback
